LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS R&G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. D/B/A R&G LOUNGE & KINSON WONG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION ADEQUATELY STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE LU V. HAWAIIAN GARDENS CASINO, INC. (2010) 50 CAL.4TH 592, 604 (THE FACT THAT THERE IS NO PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION UNDER LABOR CODE 351 "DOES NOT NECESSARILY FORECLOSE THE AVAILABILITY OF OTHER REMEDIES" INCLUDING "A COMMON LAW ACTION FOR CONVERSION").) FURTHER, PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS THAT DEFENDANTS INTERFERED WITH HIS OWNERSHIP RIGHT TO THE TIPS AND GRATUITIES, AND THAT THE AMOUNT OF MONEY IS CAPABLE OF IDENTIFICATION. (FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, 72, 73.) (SEE SOFTWARE DESIGN & APPLICATION, LTD. V. HOEFER & ARNETT, INC. (1996) 49 CAL.APP.4TH 472, 485.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AI-HUA GUAN'S, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED, MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE HAS BEEN NO MONETARY RECOVERY AND NO CLASS CERTIFICATION BECAUSE THERE IS CURRENTLY NO PLAINTIFF TO ACT AS A CLASS REPRESENTATIVE. THE COURT ALSO FINDS THAT THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS VAGUE AS IT CLEARLY REFERS TO THE RETAINER AGREEMENT OF MR. GUAN HAD WITH HIS COUNSEL. THERE IS NOTHING IN THAT AGREEMENT THAT WOULD BE IN FAVOR OF AWARDING FEES UNDER THESE CICUMSTANCES PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047 (302/MJM)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-01-2014 Vacated

Notice Of Motion: Motion To Dismiss Class Action Without Prejudice
Off Calendar Oct-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF AI-HUA GUAN, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED'S Motion To Dismiss Class Action Without Prejudice is Granted. Defendants do not oppose Plaintiff's Motion. The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR# 11185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERTO RIPAMONTI & ATTY. JASON LOHR OF THE LOHR/RIPAMONTI LLP, TEL. NO. 729-4718 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SAM W. WU OF WU & YUNG LLP, TEL. NO. 886-7103 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN K. KAO, TEL. NO. 362-9390 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF AI-HUA GUAN'S, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED, Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement is Denied. The court finds that there has been no monetary recovery and no class certification because there is currently no Plaintiff to act as a class representative. The court also finds that the settlement agreement is vague as it clearly refers to the retainer agreement of Mr. Guan had with his counsel. There is nothing in that agreement that would be in favor of awarding fees under these cicumstances Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185, Tel. No. (951)764-4047 =(302/MJM)

Request For Dismissal Of Pltf Ai-Hua Guan Pursuant To Crc 3.770
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AI-HUA GUAN, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED's request for dismissal of Plaintiff and class representative Ai-Hua Guan is granted. Plaintiffs are granted 30 days leave to amend to name a new class representative. Plaintiffs' request for limited discovery is denied. Plaintiffs previously requested this information through a special interrogatory, which Defendants objected to. (Declaration of John K. Kao, par. 6.) Plaintiffs never filed a motion to compel, and therefore, the right to compel this information is waived. (Id., par. 7.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2014 Set For Trial On Dec-15-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Continued To Mar-05-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-27-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS R&G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. D/B/A R&G LOUNGE & KINSON WONG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff's fifth cause of action adequately states a cause of action. (See Lu v. Hawaiian Gardens Casino, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 592, 604 (the fact that there is no private right of action under Labor Code 351 "does not necessarily foreclose the availability of other remedies" including "a common law action for conversion").) Further, Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts that Defendants interfered with his ownership right to the tips and gratuities, and that the amount of money is capable of identification. (First Amended Complaint, 72, 73.) (See Software Design & Application, Ltd. v. Hoefer & Arnett, Inc. (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 472, 485.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-27-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS R&G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. D/B/A R&G LOUNGE, KINSON WONG & ANGEL LIU's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. Plaintiff's eighth cause of action adequately states a cause of action. (See Lu v. Hawaiian Gardens Casino, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 592, 604 (the fact that there is no private right of action under Labor Code 351 "does not necessarily foreclose the availability of other remedies".) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
